entry.title: Login to SAP System
entry.content:
First find the SAP icon on the desktop.  

Double click on the SAP icon.

On the logon pad select the system you want to login to and press log on button.

Enter Client, User, password and press enter.

This is SAP easy access screen. All the tools required by ABAP developer is under the node Tools.

entry.title: Debugging SAPscripts form in SAP
entry.content:
To activate SAP Script debugger:

Step 1:

Go to Transaction SE38.
Enter the program name RSTXDBUG


Press Execute.

Step 2:

Go to SE71.

In the initial screen, Enter the form name and click on Utilities --> Activate Debugger

entry.title: ADS Server installation check in SAP
entry.content:
The first Steps while developing Adobe forms is to check which ADS server is installed and what is the version of the ADS installed in sap.

To check weather the system has ADS installed or not we can run the various standard programs below.

FP_PDF_TEST_00

This program can be used to check the ADS installed version. If the program gives an error. It means that you SAP system has some connection problems with the ADS Server and you might need help of Basis member.

This above screen shows the successful ADS connection with SAP system.

FP_CHECK_DESTINATION_SERVICE Used to check the Destination service to ADS.

Successful data transfer

FP_CHECK_HTTP_DATA_TRANSFER The above program can be used to can also be used to check the http data transfer

FP_TEST_00 This program is can used to test the pdf file generation.

where LOCL is the printer device setup. By default LP01 can also be used. If any error persists in device please contact your Basis team for help. If all the connection is setup properly the pdf file will be generated successfully. Or else you might get the exception as the below image:

Note: Without proper ADS setup in SAP you will not be able to activate the FORM in SFP transaction
